title: STANDARD FOR MAIZE (CORN) CXS 153-1985 Adopted in 1985. Revised in 1995. Amended in 2019.

sections.section: STANDARD FOR MAIZE (CORN) CXS 153-1985 Adopted in 1985. Revised in 1995. Amended in 2019.
sections.summary: Document title: Codex Standard for Maize (Corn) CXS 153-1985, adopted 1985, revised 1995 and amended 2019.

Scope: Applies to maize (corn) for human consumption presented ready for use (packaged or sold loose), specifies requirements for whole grain shelled dent and/or shelled flint maize (Zea mays indentata/indurata L.) and their hybrids, and explicitly excludes processed maize.
Product definition: Maize (corn) is defined as the shelled grains of the species specified in the scope.

General quality requirements: Maize must be safe and suitable for human consumption and free from abnormal flavours, odours and living insects, and free from filth in amounts that may represent a hazard to human health.

Specific quality factors: Maximum moisture 15.5% m/m (with lower limits possibly required for some destinations); extraneous matter is defined and limited, with filth (animal-origin impurities including dead insects) max 0.1% m/m, toxic or noxious seeds (e.g., Crotalaria, Agrostemma, Ricinus, Datura) must be absent in hazardous amounts, other organic extraneous matter max 1.5% m/m, and inorganic extraneous matter max 0.5% m/m.

Packaging requirements: Maize shall be packaged in containers that safeguard hygienic, nutritional, technological and organoleptic qualities; containers and materials must be safe and not impart toxic substances or undesirable odours/flavours; sacks must be clean, sturdy and strongly sewn or sealed.

Labelling additions: In addition to the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods, the product name must be shown as 'maize (corn)', and for non-retail containers the name of the product, lot identification and the manufacturer/packer name and address must appear on the container or accompanying documents (lot ID and name/address may be replaced by an identifiable mark linked to the documents).
Name of the product: The label must show the product name 'maize (corn)'.

Methods of analysis and sampling: Compliance with the Standard shall be checked using the relevant methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999).

Annex: Users should specify the appropriate factor limits and analytical methods when more than one is given; the Annex sets numeric limits and methods for kernel colour (e.g., yellow maize: other colours max 5.0% by weight; white maize: other colours max 2.0%), kernel shape (other shapes max 5.0% for flint or dent; flint/dent mix range 5–95% flint), and defects (blemished grains max 7.0% with diseased ≤0.5%, broken kernels max 6.0% using ISO 5223‑1983 4.50 mm sieve, other grains max 2.0%), determined primarily by visual examination or specified methods.
